one_time_description: Impairment write-down on investment in Great Lakes Hospitality Group Limited Partnership
economic_notes: First Nation has 51% interest in Helios Whitefish River Limited Partnership (portfolio method), 8.3% interest in Mnidoo Mnising Power Limited Partnership (portfolio method), and 17.6% interest in Great Lakes Hospitality Group Limited Partnership (hotel and conference centre on Manitoulin Island). Fully consolidates Whitefish River Development Corporation and Birch Island Construction Company.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2020. Province is Ontario (Manitoulin Island area). Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Whitefish River Development Corporation and Birch Island Construction Company. One GBE: Great Lakes Hospitality Group Limited Partnership (equity method, 17.6% interest, impaired). Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($4,663,985) + CMHC ($1,590,793) + Government of Canada other ($524,631) + Ontario ($1,676054) + OFNLP ($934,049) + Anishinabek organizations ($4,253,531) = $13,643,043; however Anishinabek organizations may not be government transfers — if excluded, total government transfers = $9,389,512 and OSR = $6,026,883. Used full sum including Anishinabek as they appear to be transfer organizations. No schedule of remuneration included in the document. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event uncertainty. Loans payable includes ISC promissory note of $833,790 for boundary claim. Deferred revenue includes prepaid lease revenue of $14,240. Annual debt service includes repayment of long-term debt ($254,092) and loans payable ($5,903) = $259,995.
